entry.title: Re: How can I change the Fiori group display setting for all users to show one group per tab?

Hello,

We are facing same issue. We did configuration as the screenshot below but Main Page still shows all groups in one page.

 

But if we change user setting to "Show one group at a time" , the page works as we want perfectly. How can we set this setting for all users?

entry.title: Comparing SAP Analytics Cloud Planning Security vs. OneStream Security: A Comprehensive Analysis
entry.content:
In the modern landscape of enterprise performance management (EPM) and analytics, ensuring robust security is paramount. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Planning and OneStream are two leading platforms offering powerful tools for planning, forecasting, and financial analysis. However, when it comes to security, how do these two giants compare? This blog will delve into a detailed comparison of the security features of SAP Analytics Cloud Planning and OneStream, providing insights for businesses aiming to make an informed decision.

 

 

 

 

Overview of SAP Analytics Cloud Planning Security

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is a comprehensive solution that combines business intelligence, planning, and predictive analytics in a single cloud environment. Security in SAC Planning is deeply integrated into the platform, ensuring data protection, user access control, and compliance with global security standards.

  1. Data Encryption

    • At Rest: SAC employs robust encryption mechanisms for data at rest, using AES-256 encryption, which is a widely recognized industry standard.
    • In Transit: All data transmitted between the client and server is encrypted using TLS (Transport Layer Security) protocols, ensuring secure communication.

  2. User Authentication and Access Control

    • Single Sign-On (SSO): SAC supports SSO through SAML 2.0, allowing users to access the platform seamlessly while ensuring secure authentication.
    • Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): SAC offers detailed role-based access controls, enabling administrators to define permissions based on user roles, ensuring that sensitive data is only accessible to authorized personnel.

  3. Data Privacy and Compliance

    • GDPR Compliance: SAC is compliant with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), providing tools for data anonymization and user consent management.
    • Data Centers: SAC operates on a network of secure data centers worldwide, ensuring data residency and compliance with regional regulations.

  4. Audit and Monitoring

    • SAC provides comprehensive audit logs that track user activities, including data access and modifications. This feature is critical for maintaining transparency and ensuring accountability within the platform.

Overview of OneStream Security

OneStream, a unified platform for financial planning and consolidation, also offers a strong security framework designed to protect sensitive financial data and ensure compliance.

  1. Data Encryption

    • At Rest: OneStream uses encryption techniques like AES-256 to protect data stored within the platform.
    • In Transit: Similar to SAC, OneStream ensures data security during transmission through TLS protocols.

  2. User Authentication and Access Control

    • SSO and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA): OneStream supports SSO and also offers MFA options, adding an extra layer of security to the authentication process.
    • Security Profiles: OneStream’s security model includes detailed security profiles that control user access at various levels, including application, dimension, and data level.

  3. Data Privacy and Compliance

    • Regulatory Compliance: OneStream is compliant with various global regulations, including GDPR, ensuring that the platform meets stringent data protection standards.
    • Security Certifications: OneStream holds certifications like SOC 2 Type II, demonstrating its commitment to maintaining a secure environment.

  4. Audit and Monitoring

    • OneStream offers extensive logging and monitoring capabilities, allowing organizations to track changes, access history, and maintain an audit trail for compliance purposes.

 

SAC Application Security

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) securities revolve around user management, roles, and teams. Users are assigned roles that determine their access to data and functionalities. Roles are typically categorized by business needs and linked to specific permissions. SAC also emphasizes organizing content within secure folders and projects to maintain control over data access. Best practices include avoiding default folders, structuring content based on projects, and regularly reviewing user roles to ensure they align with organizational policies.

OneStream Application Security

OneStream security best practices highlight the importance of a layered security approach. It is advised to set up detailed security profiles to control user access at different levels, including application, dimension, and data. Best practices include regularly reviewing and updating security profiles, using Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for enhanced protection, and carefully managing access permissions based on user roles and responsibilities. Maintaining securities thorough audit logs and monitoring user activities are crucial for ensuring compliance and security.

 

Conclusion

Choosing between SAP Analytics Cloud Planning and OneStream requires careful consideration of your organization’s security needs. Both platforms offer strong security measures, but differences in authentication options, compliance certifications, and user access management may sway your decision. Ultimately, your choice should align with your company’s specific security requirements and regulatory obligations.

Whether you prioritize the comprehensive role-based controls of SAP Analytics Cloud or the enhanced authentication features of OneStream, both platforms are well-equipped to protect your critical business data.

entry.title: Re: Understanding Job Profile Acknowledgment in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Thanks for sharing Sachin! Very insightful!!

entry.title: Re: To stop Email notifications related to workitems
entry.content:
Hi Govindu,

The email may be sent by a job running called Extended Notifications. This sends an email for every workitem(workflow) sent to users. Details below.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sending-mail-using-extended-notification/ba-p/13284214

entry.title: Re: PresentationVariants on Object Page Table
entry.content: 2 years later and I have the same question.

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve daily work calendar from EC Timesheet...
entry.content:
Hi, 

not quite clear what you want to achieve. We have currently only internal apis that query the planned working time, no public available APIs. However, you can report on the planned working time if this helps - check my last blog where I explained this.

But if you just need the planned working time for whatever reasons in the integration center there are multiple options:

1) Make this employees to negative time recorders. If they don´t record anything on a day the planned working time is used as recorded working time. And you can create then with 1 simple time valuation a time type group set as pay type. This is then stored on the database and you have the data per any day in the integration center as time valuation result.

2) You can also fill a time container. This should have the same effect. But probably most easiest is Option 1

best regards

Volker

entry.title: Re: RCM and RMK usage/compatibility question
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Thank you for answering. So, Company A will be using RCM for the HR Management Side, then use RMK for External Facing/Career Site. Company B will be using RCM for the HR Management side, but wants to use the old/standard/native RCM Career Site (They are not ready to move to RMK).

We do not know if this is possible. As far as we can see and experience, RMK is bleeding over to the standard/native RCM Career Site.

I hope my explanation is clear.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Welcome @Beykan - that's great to hear!
There is a location group set up by community members, for Istanbul in case you are interested to join that? Members such as @barisguler are posting about local events there. 

You might also be interested to read about the Devtoberfest contest and the SAP TechEd virtual event that takes place in October this year.

entry.title: Four questions to ask when adding CDC, CDP and Emarsys to your marketing toolset
entry.content:
Coming off another disappointing loss to the Kansas City Chiefs in the NFL Super Bowl LVIII earlier this year, San Francisco 49ers football head coach Kyle Shanahan offered his perspective on the team’s mentality going into the offseason:

You look at (every option) every single year and you try to become the best team possible with the situation that you're in. You don't just say, 'Hey, we're upgrading here this year.' You've got to make sure that's available. And if it's not, then you get stronger in a different area. There's lots of ways you can win. You just have to keep trying to find that way.

This is a puzzle that teams across all professional sports leagues are trying to solve as they return from an offseason of free agent signings, trades and transactions. How will new personnel fit into our organization and how do we optimize their strengths? What kind of process and platform changes will new leadership and their processes bring? How do these changes move the needle and bring us closer to our season goals?

One well-known NHL sports team is asking these questions not only about their team but also recent changes to their marketing technology.

Over the past three summers, this customer took steps to optimize their business’ approach to customer marketing with the addition of three new SAP systems–Customer Data Cloud (CDC), Customer Data Platform (CDP) and Emarsys–and have been monitoring performance of select email campaigns ever since. These solutions replaced a longtime player in their CX architecture–SAP Marketing Cloud (SMC)–which the customer had been using for years create segments of users across multiple sources and market to them with personalized email communications.

A system overhaul like this requires not only business investment in the new technology, but also team training, solution alignment and periodic assessment. From discovery through launch, the customer kept in mind the following questions–ones that any company should consider when planning a shift from a single platform to a multi-cloud solution.

What value do we get from the trade?

While SMC helped the team with email marketing needs, members were never able to take full advantage of the platform due, in part, to platform limitations. With the SAP acquisition of CDC (formerly “Gigya”) in late 2017 and Emarsys in 2020, along with the release of CDP in 2020, the customer saw an opportunity to improve their marketing solution and leverage strengths from each platform:

  • CDC – for capturing user marketing consent, newsletter opt-ins and customer data through Screen-Sets and Hosted Pages.
  • CDP – for handling complex merging, segmentation and orchestration with systems based on data privacy permissions.
  • Emarsys – for marketing automation, improved workflows and customer experience.

How do new players fit within the current system?

For the initial two program phases, the customer focused on replacing SMC with a core CX framework that leverages CDC and DSP as data sources, paving the way for future integrations. 

CX Marketing Architecture including CDC, CDP and Emarsys

 By shifting from SMC to a multi-cloud solution, the customer can get the most value out of each system while not being restricted to just one platform. The flexibility and agility gained through this approach will be key as the customer looks to connect to other data sources, both from SAP and outside of SAP.

How well does each player know their role?

With multiple solutions, however, comes a new question around overlapping features and functions.

Just like in any team with talented players, there are bound to be some systems with overlapping capabilities. There isn’t one solution that fits all, so it’s important to invest time to clearly define the roles of each application and decide which platform is best suited for handling day-to-day tasks.

Take, for example, segmentation. Both CDP and Emarsys platforms have tools to segment users, so which system should customers use to build the segment?

For this customer, the direction largely depended on answers to a few questions:

  • What are the business use cases / campaigns that we are looking to launch in this phase?
  • What data needs to be available in each system to carry out the campaigns?
  • What system would be easier for a non-technical marketing team to use and maintain?

In the end, the solution that the customer landed on involved a combination of the two platforms:

  • Emarsys for non-complex segmentation needs (e.g. subscribed to a newsletter or are part of a specific tier). This requires that any permanent, re-usable contact information from CDP is synced with Emarsys.
  • CDP for more advanced field calculations and segmentation (e.g. users who purchased tickets to X game) and send trigger events to Emarsys when email (e.g. pre-event, post-event) should be sent to the user.

How are we going to develop our players and team?

The goal for the development staffing for any professional sports team is to try and turn players into serviceable assets. The same can be said for new marketing technologies.

The customer is already looking forward to the next integrations that require more advanced capabilities of CDC, CDP and Emarsys and expand the dataset coming from DSP. With a myriad of other data sources available in their network, from parking reservations and restaurant purchases to storefront and arena (Wi-Fi) logins, there are many ways to further develop the solution and personalize customer communication, which in turn breeds loyal customers.

It won’t happen overnight, of course. Developing a multi-cloud marketing solution requires thoughtful planning, iterative updates, and commitment. While this customer’s off-season marketing technology changes marked a big step in the right direction, there is still plenty of work ahead to realize their full marketing vision.

entry.title: Re: Signavio Process Intelligence and ServiceNow Integration
entry.content:
Hi,

what exactly are you searching for?

When I go to Process Intelligence, I can create a connection to ServiceNow by entering the following data:

 

That's actually the documentation, that you already linked, which you can share with customers: https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-intelligence/user-guide/connector-servicenow?locale=en-US 

Maybe I didn't understand what kind of prerequisites you are looking for 🙂

Cheers,
Dennis

entry.title: How to change the destination stock type of a HU - Usage of BAdI /SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_CAT
entry.content:
Requirement:During internal transfer posting of a Full HU (/SCWM/ADHU) or a Product transfer( /SCWM/ADPROD) using Fiori or RF transaction, there shall be a capability of switching the destination stock type from its source one. If source HU has stock type has F2, the destination stock type of HU should be Q4 or B6. This is primarily used in industry where HU or partial HU stock will undergo Tempering or Thawing process( A kind of Quarantine for specific Hour),a process of improving the state or characteristics  of the product. After certain hours when tempering time is over, the Stock type should be automatically changed from QQ to FF in the same storage type via background job reading HU custom able

Problem Addressed/ Standard Scenario: There is no standard functionality available in SAP which allows to flip the destination HU stock type on the fly when it is internally moved from one storage type A to destination Storage type B. One way is to hard code the 'Non Location Independent Stock Type'' QQ or BB in the destination storage type. However, the major pitfall is the storage type will never allow the posting change from QQ or BB to FF in Standard.

Solution: This blog will showcase a solution how to flip the stock type even hard coding the the 'Non Location Independent Stock Type'' QQ or BB to F2 in config .Also, there is no Badi available in EWM landscape which allows to change destination stock type at Warehouse task level. In the past there was BADI /SCWM/EX_WHO_DSTGRP which allowed to change everything on WT level, this door is now closed unfortunately. 

The above issue is raised in customer connection as well.

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence (sap.com)

However, there is workaround of flipping the stock type using Badi /SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_CAT.  This BAdI Allows  to determine the destination stock type in the case of automatic posting change upon warehouse task confirmation.

The current logic of stock type change is based on certain condition when a HU is internally moved from Storage type A to Storage Type B( QQ Hard code), system will flip the stock type from FF to QQ which is all standard. However after quarantine hour is over, the posting change from Monitor, /SCWM/POST, using  program /SCWM/R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE or using FM /SCWM/STOCK_CHANGE  allows to change the stock type from QQ to FF using Badi BAdI /SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_CAT. 

In Standard, once storage type stock category is hard coded, it never allows to switch from QQ to FF or BB. System will always give an error message stock type change is not allowed. Hence, the above BAdI helps to ve multiple stock type even storage type allows single Stock category.

Storage type Config: The storage type has stock category as 'QQ'. Here QQ stock category is without QM. Customer can also chose BB if HU needs some rest time before it is switched back to FF.

In nutshell, the solution revolves into 2 aspects.

  1. Movement of HU from X to Y storage type and posting change from FF to QQ via Storage type config
  2. Posting change of HU from QQ to FF, the destination stock type change upon WT confirmation along with BAdI /SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_CAT to skip hard coded configuration.

  1. Perform a internal Transfer of an HU

The stock type changes to Q4 based on standard configuration.

Perform the stock type change from Q4 to F2 via Warehouse Monitor considering tempering time is over.

 

Post BAdI implementation, system would allow to flip the stock type disregarding storage type configuration.

Badi details:

To allow the scope of this BAdI only for tempering storage type, we have added an external switch so to influence the stock type change specific for tempering storage type . For other storage type, the logic will not influence and execute as per standard logic.

Below is the switch created which is public attribute and has been utilized externally to set 'X'.

Thanks for reading.

The blog has been co-authored by @prem_swaroop from SAP.

entry.title: Re: VBAK - Table activation error

You can find the usage of field with where used list.

Try removing the usage of field from table maintenance or usage in programs if any and activate it.

Thanks,

Prakash J.

entry.title: Re: Read/validate xml namespace using groovy script in SA...
entry.content: Are you only interested in the default namespace or you want to know all of them? The //namespace::* would give all namespaces, but you can use namespace-uri() to get the information of only the default namespace.

I cannot preview my app on my device only laptop preview. Everytime i enter the pin for preview it says session expired and reloads. 

I dont know how to set my server location or what ever it is like US10 ect. It says i do not have a "global account" when i log in and try to go to my "cockpit". Im pretty far into my development but im worried i need to address these issues now before I put this whole SAP stuff down for good.

entry.title: Re: Regarding the 'Dispatch time' settings for output type...
entry.content:
Hi Chandra, please help me with to clarify my doubt, if dispatch time is set to "Send with application own transaction", then we have too manually maintained the output type in document, but if I set the dispatch time to "send immediately" then output time is maintained at the time of saving the document.

I created the Sub Con Challan, and I have to maintain the output type in VF02 T-code after that I can preview the document (Dispatch time is "Send with application own transaction"), but I set the Dispatch time to "send immediately" then I skip the VF02 process to maintaining the output or not ?

entry.title: Re: #COE #BA01 Schedule Post Closing and Opening Balances...
entry.content:
Hola P_Quiroz_23 

According to the case description, I think you are talking about the Balance Carryforward job automatically. If this is the case, the balance carryforward job is scheduled for approx. Two weeks before the fiscal year closes. This job, which is not visible to the users, is executed every night after midnight. This job checks, for each company code and ledger combination, whether a balance carryforward job should be scheduled for this company code and ledger combination.


However, it is possible to run the job from the Schedule General ledger job app manually, but you would wait until all periods of the previous fiscal year are closed to avoid such a situation. This means that a manual balance carryforward is only possible after the start of the next fiscal year. And every journal entry, which was already handled by the automatic balance carryforward, is ignored. The system won’t duplicate postings.
The Balance Carry Forward Status app provides the planned, in-process, or completed status.

More details in the following links:

- 3014633 - Technical Background to Automatic Scheduling of Balance Carryforward Runs

- SAP Help Portal Balance Carryforward

Best regards

Edna

entry.title: Re: RAP Application Previewing Error in VSCode: Cannot fi...
entry.content: how about upgrading all the lib to latest in your package.json?

entry.title: Reuse of sap:label in sap cap application with external services
entry.content:
As CAP developers, we often rely on external web services, many of which are provided by S/4HANA in either OData v2 or v4.

One advantage is that S/4HANA services already include translated labels for various entities and properties.

For example, if I’m creating an entity in my CAP application that is associated with a plant, I would like to reuse the sap:label from the relevant S/4HANA service property, rather than manually defining and translating a local label in my application.

How can I dynamically reuse these SAP-provided labels in my CAP application to avoid redundant translations?

entry.title: Failed to generate the backend operation proxy for RFC
entry.content:
When I try to generate the odata of my project I am faced with this error and no matter what I did it won't generate, I don't have any open requests that includes another entity of the odata
I have tried to remap the entity that uses that function module and removed all the variables that uses the data type P and still no luck any help

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Inspection lot creation on Sales Return Ins...
entry.content:
Is it this one?

3271931 - Inspection lot with origin 17 is created despite QM is deactivated for movement type in OMJJ

Resolution

In order to restrict inspection lot creation in EWM environment, following possibilities can be considered:

A) Use EWM inspection rule to restrict the lot creation for certain document type, item type or other properties. In decentralized EWM landscape, inspection rules are used by default. In embedded EWM landscape, the setting to use inspection rules can be reached at following customizing: transaction SPRO ---> SCM Extended Warehouse Management ---> Extended Warehouse Management ---> Cross-Process Settings ---> Quality Management ---> Basic and Integration ---> Define and Activate Warehouse-Dependent IOTs.

Inspection rules can be maintained in transaction /SCWM/QRSETUP.

B) Check if BAdI /SCWM/EX_QM_INSP_RELEVANCE (method: INSPECTION_RELEVANCE_SET) can be used to prevent inspection lot creation according to the business requirement. This BAdI is available for both decentralized and embedded landscape.

entry.title: Re: VBAK - Table activation error

Martin Pösinger

entry.title: How can I add a "floating field" to the table?
entry.content:
Hi Masters,

My friends who developed before me added text fields / floating fields ({Packing}, {FKIMG}, {TOT_VAL}, {WAERK}) to the table. AdobeForms recognized these added floating fields.

 



Similarly, when I want to add a floating field ({ZMAN_C}) to the table, it does not accept it.

 

How can I add a "floating field" to the table? Can you help?

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve daily work calendar from EC Timesheet...
entry.content: Hi Volker, You have solved our problem in one go. "negative time recorders. If they don´t record anything on a day the planned working time is used as recorded working time" - we were not aware of this, but this is the key to the answer. Once we changed it to negative, the employee's standard working hours from the Work Schedule were instantly available in the employeeTime OData object. Very much appreciated - thank you. Kind regards / LG, Alan

entry.title: Re: Database Initialization Failed
entry.content:
Hello.
Were you capable of solving this error?
Kind regards

entry.title: Re: To stop Email notifications related to workitems
entry.content:
Hi Daley,

Thanks for response. I have gone through that mentioned blog. But I didn’t find anywhere to delete or exclude the user mail ID and User is getting only old workflow items 2021 workflow items. and those emails are coming from workflow batch and receipients are 9 users out of 9 users 2 mail id’s need to remove and those two users don’t want the email remainders so is there anyway to exclude the user mail id’s.

when system is sending the email reminders to the related workflow items purchase documents, it means some where work flow items got stucked in the year of 2021. Thats why they are getting the email notifications.

Today we found workflow related table. In that table i filtered the 2021 workflow items with Task id and user id, we got all the workflow items related to user id and all those workflow items are in the year of 2021 and exported and sent to the user. Once i got confirmation from user for deletion of those work items. Then we will delete all those 2021 year work items. and lets seee whether the issue will resolve or not

entry.content:
I would not recommend creating a custom report -- please check standard SAP reports information system > logistics > inventory management> material and explore all like MC.9,MC.A,MC.B & MC.C 

if S4 -- then 

Dead Stock Analysis -F2899
Inventory Turnoever-F1956
Inventory KPI Analysis-F3749

entry.title: Re: TS-Based Supply Optimizer finishes before Maximum Solving Runtime with Technical Gap (1 iteratio
Re: How to hide a view completely in s4hana public cloud...
entry.content:
Hello @sunilpadhy 

 

The Business Catalog SAP_CMD_BC_PR_MAINT_PC that is need to access the Create Material app 

has a restriction 'Maintenance Status of Material' where you control the views displayed on user authorization 

 

If you need the basic data views hidden then you could maintain the maintenance statuses , without adding K

within a role and assign it to the user

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: How can I prevent the "delete" key from being deleted from the alv?
entry.content: Thank you @Sandra_Rossi.

entry.title: Re: How to hide a view completely in s4hana public cloud for Material Management?
entry.content: adding tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud Master Data

entry.title: Re: TS-Based Supply Optimizer finishes before Maximum Sol...
entry.content:
Hi Vincent,

If the optimizer finished before your Maximum Solving Time, it means the optimizer found an optimal solution within 2,500 seconds. This is true for any discrete and linear run: if the optimizer finds the optimal solution (i.e. solution gap < 10e-6), it will stop before reaching the Max Solving Time.

You might want to test increasing your product decomposition's window size because it might be too small and the optimizer is solving it too fast.

Best regards,
Matheus

entry.title: Re: Work center not allowed for plant maintenance
entry.content: What is usage you have on the Work Center --make sure it is 009-All Task Types

entry.title: Re: Hide Save Button from Global toolbar in MDG
entry.content:
You can check the method CHECK_ACTION_USAGE_SINGLE of class CL_USMD_CR_MASTER, and then call the method modify_cnr_button with respective parameters. 

We used the above to achieve disabling of submit, approve and reject buttons based on business requirements.

entry.content:
@CarlosRoggan - Great blog Carlos. However, how can this be applied to non-AD domain users? Like service users (in terms of SAP language) rather than a domain or named user?

@BrendanFarthing - Did you get an answer to your question? We are in the similar boat and looking to have AAD authenticate the users for them to post to an iflow on SAP-IS. We also want to restrict the users to be able to post to the iflow only they are supposed to and not to any others, just by guessing the URLs for what so ever reason.

entry.title: Re: ATP check for sto purchase orders
entry.content: Check SAP note 2195518

entry.title: OpenDocument String to Stored Procedure Universe
entry.content:
To invoke reports from our application we use the OpenDocument interface. Some report parameters allow for multiple selections. In the OpenDocument url, multiple values for a parameter are specified by separating each value with a semicolon, and the parameter is prefixed with lsM (to specify that the parameter supports multiple values). The problem occurs with some of our WebI reports that use a Stored Procedure universe (sp_unv). Our stored procedures use a varchar stored procedure parameter to receive the multiple values, a single string that contains the different values separated by semicolons. However, it seems SAP BO 4.3 is unable to map the multiple values to a stored procedure parameter of nvarchar type. 

While we can change the parameter prefix to lsS, and pass all the values to the stored procedure as a single string, the problem is that we use the same variable in other native WebI queries too. We can modify our application code to specify variables that support multiple values twice, and append "AsStr" to the parameter name. Then modify the universe to map stored procedures parameters to the "AsStr" prompt variable. For example, if ProviderID supports multiple values, we can append two parameters to the OpenDocument url, one called lsMProviderID=1;2;3 and another one called lsSProviderIDAsStr=1;2;3. The stored procedure parameter in the stored proc universe would then be changed from ProviderID to ProviderIDAsStr.

We will contact SAP support to see if there is another way to handle this, maybe there is a backwards compatible setting that would behave the same as SAP 4.2, and pass multiple values to the stored procedure as a string (nvarchar)? We did not seem to have this issue with version 4.2. By passing the values separated by ";" it would pass the list to one query, and send it as a string to the stored procedure universe.

entry.title: Re: How to use 'REUSE_ALV_FIELDCATALOG_MERGE'
entry.content: No, if I declear the internal table with TYPE instead of LIKE, it gives me an error, saying that particular structure is not of any internal table. It only works with standard tables.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page

entry.title: Top Reasons to Attend SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
SAP TechEd is a high-energy, interactive virtual event experience, which is accessible to all. With a jam-packed agenda and some of the top tech topics AND speakers – there really are very few reasons why NOT to attend! The overall SAP TechEd program journey provides many opportunities to the individual attendee and by browsing the Session Catalog in advance, you have time – not only to choose your sessions of interest carefully – but also to prepare your questions in advance for the interactive sessions!

One of our top leaders and contributors across SAP Community and ecosystem is SAP Champion, Kartik Dua. Learn from Kartik how to supercharge your career at SAP TechEd: boost your skills, strengthen your role, and explore new horizons.

Kartik Dua (USA) | SAP Champion since 2019 | Company: Xylem | Number of SAP TechEd events attended: 3.
In the ever-evolving world of technology, staying ahead of the curve is essential for any tech professional. Whether you're an SAP expert, developer, architect, or just beginning your journey in tech, SAP TechEd offers a unique opportunity to supercharge your career. Here's how SAP TechEd can help you advance, become more effective in your role, and expand your technical education into exciting new fields like AI.

What You Will Learn to Advance Your Career

SAP TechEd is more than just a tech conference; it's a career-defining experience. At this event, you'll have access to the latest knowledge and trends in enterprise technology. From hands-on workshops and expert-led sessions to deep dives into SAP's innovative solutions, you'll acquire valuable insights that can help you stay competitive in the fast-paced tech landscape.

You'll learn about emerging technologies such as AI, machine learning, cloud computing, and data management that are reshaping industries. By mastering these skills, you'll position yourself as an innovator within your organization, opening doors to new opportunities and career growth.

Sharpen Your Skills for Success in Your Role

Beyond the big-picture career benefits, SAP TechEd provides highly practical learning experiences that will enhance your day-to-day performance. Through technical workshops, real-world case studies, and in-depth training, you’ll gain hands-on expertise to improve your job effectiveness.

You'll walk away with practical solutions to optimize your use of SAP technology, streamline processes, and overcome complex challenges within your organization. This knowledge will not only make you more competent but also more confident in your ability to drive positive outcomes in your role.

Expand Your Technical Education into new Fields Like AI

One of the most exciting aspects of SAP TechEd is the opportunity to broaden your technical skillset. In today's digital economy, cross-functional expertise is increasingly important. At SAP TechEd, you can explore new fields like artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning, and automation—technologies that are revolutionizing the business world.

By expanding your knowledge in these areas, you'll not only enhance your personal skill set but also help future-proof your career. AI and related technologies are becoming central to how organizations operate, and having expertise in these fields can make you indispensable in your company’s digital journey.

Why SAP TechEd Should Be Your Next Stop

SAP TechEd is a unique event where you’ll grow not just as a professional but also as a thought leader. You’ll have the chance to connect with like-minded peers, industry pioneers, and SAP experts who are pushing the boundaries of enterprise technology. The insights and skills you’ll gain here will have a lasting impact on your career trajectory.

Whether your goal is to climb the ranks, sharpen your current skills, or explore the future of technology, SAP TechEd offers the knowledge and network to help you succeed. Make SAP TechEd your next move and get ready to shape the future of your career in tech.

Kartik Dua (SAP Champion)

More Reasons to Attend

Kunal Bansal (India) | SAP Champion since 2023 | Company: IBM India | Number of TechEd events attended: 2.
- Connect with SAP Mentors, SAP Champions, experts, developers, and thought leaders to build connections.
- Discover the latest innovations driving the digital transformation, including SAP Joule, AI-assisted integrations, and SAP BTP.

Tammy Powlas (USA) | SAP Mentor since 2010 | Company: Fairfax Water| Number of SAP TechEd events attended: 14.
- See how SAP is executing what was discussed at Sapphire and help plan for 2025.  “It’s not about marketing, but what the product can do”.
- It’s free, no travel.

Gregor Wolf (Germany) | SAP Mentor since 2007 | Company: Computerservice Wolf | Number of SAP TechEd events attended: Way too many to count!
- Dive deep with the updated hands-on tutorials.
- Use the live chat to address questions directly to SAP product owners, product management or developers.

Ankur Godre (UK) | SAP Mentor since 2015 | Company: LTIMindtree | Number of SAP TechEd events attended: 3.
- Get insights into what the future holds:
Keep yourselves abreast of the latest and greatest in the SAP world (Joule, Business AI and more) and get deep insights for planning your organization’s tech strategy with the unmissable keynotes providing insights into what's coming in the future, the technology trends, product launches, key announcements and SAP's strategic direction.
- Learning, Certification and Career advancement:
No tech event is of value if you are not learning something new. Learn through several hands-on practical and tailored sessions of your choice, get certified onsite with a bonus advantage of discounted price, and use the knowledge and certification gained to advance your career further.
- Networking with Industry peers/experts and inspiration from success stories:
Build productive relationships & share experiences with professionals from varied industries and organizations, whether you are a developer, architect, business leader or partner. Finally, there is no better validation than to hear directly from customers who have successfully implemented different SAP solutions to solve their business problems. Get inspiration and ideas to write your own success story!

With all these top reasons, we know you won’t want to miss out on this great opportunity to attend. Remember – it’s free! - join our experts and create your own tech adventure.

Register now for SAP TechEd
Connect with our SAP Champions and Mentors via our directory.
Check out the SAP Champions and Mentors expert session playlists and view their recommendations from the SAP TechEd virtual catalog.
Stay tuned for a blog coming soon on how to prepare for and make the best out of your SAP Teched experience.

Co-authors @Kartik_Dua @KunalBansal @TammyPowlas @gregorw @ankurgodre

any guidance is greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Business Server Page (BSP) error
entry.content:
Hello Manuel,

can you try the /ets/hosts file mapping as desribed in the Getting Started Guide ? The CRM UIs usually don't work with regular IP addresses in the URL.

Best, Joerg

entry.title: Re: TS-Based Supply Optimizer finishes before Maximum Sol...
entry.content:
Hi @mkorndoerfer Thank you for your quick reply.

Is 10e-6 indeed the cut-off value for an optimal solution?
Since this number is crucial, could you please elaborate on the notation?

Are we talking about 10×10^(-6) here = 1x10^(-5) = 0.00001 ?

In this case, then our 2.7e-05 will be larger (2.7 times larger) isn't it?

Since 2.7e-05 = 2.7x10^(-5) = 0.000027.

entry.title: Re: How Time Zone inconsistencies can result in no data available for SAP Cloud ALM Operations use c
entry.content:
Additionally the time zone settings are checked every time you call /sdf/alm_setup.

If a problem is detected it will be reported

entry.title: Re: REST vs. OData in SAP PO
entry.content:
I assume JSON is the only way forward. The adapter should translate from XML.
And batch processing is the only way to go, to my opinion. We need to be able to process multiple messages, in 1 big source message, in 1 shot.
If not, we face issues with the sequence

What do you mean with "if the payload has only json data"? If you go for batch processing in the adapter, the choice for Atom or JSON disappears

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )

Data Preview of the CDS View Entity which contains,

  1. 1 new field with concatenated values of Total_Price and Currency_Code.
  2. 1 new field with Overall Status Text

entry.title: Re: REST vs. OData in SAP PO
entry.content: let me ask this way, do you know batch? do you know how to use batch?

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP Optimizer doesn't give a solution while the i...
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

Based on the optimizer message "can't perform sourcing for missing supply of X,XX in time, because of the planning horizon.", it seems like your lead times are too long and the FG won't be available to satisfy the demand on time.

Could you please check what's all the transportation and production lead times involved, and validate if it's really sufficient or not to fulfill your independent demand on time?

As the message suggests, it probably isn't. So either you adjust your lead times, allow late delivery, or move your independent demands to a later date.

Best regards,
Matheus

entry.title: Re: TS-Based Supply Optimizer finishes before Maximum Sol...

We want to share using organization rather than assigning each user.

Any pointers .. please.

 

Thanks,

Raghav

entry.title: Re: is it possible to install SAP Cloud ALM no cloud but...
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM will not be offered as an on-premise solution.
However planned for 2025 is the Sovereign Cloud Data Center for customers who require high levels of security,
An overview can be found under Technical Information at the SAP Cloud ALM homepage.
Nevertheless, there is no clear timeline for this. Therefore, customers which require an application lifecycle
management solution can continue using SAP Solution Manager (which is available in NS2 for example) to
implement and operate their solution. Or you can continue to operate your own on-premise SAP Solution Manager system.

Customers will be able to transition to SAP Cloud ALM when a Sovereign
Cloud offering is available in their region and based on their own schedule.

Perhaps this helps?

entry.title: Re: Reuse of sap:label in sap cap application with external services

entry.title: Re: How to Update Table ARBCIG_SYSTIDMAP With BAPI or FM
entry.content: Search BAPI transaction code to search for released BAPI. If you can't find one then do a /h to go into debug mode before starting the transaction. Then you can step through the code and discover the various classes and function modules. You can create your own BAPI or FM using those classes.

entry.title: Re: Which website is the best to learn a SAPUI5 javascrip...
entry.content: Right. The Javascript training is a bit different than the JS code in SAPUI5 training videos. To be honest, most of SAPUI5 courses use "old" JS syntax. Though, I think it's good to know all.

entry.title: Re: SAML2 service not accessibleWhat has happened?Calling...
entry.content:
Hi Azza,

If the below doesn't help, feel free to provide some further context on the environments. How are you trying to call exactly? Using a proxy server? Web dispatcher?

One general remark/requirement is that end-users need to access the Service Provider in the same way that the Identity Provider itself will contact it when sending the SAML 2.0 response (use the same host name, protocol and port).

For example, you might experience the error due to any of the following mismatches:

  • Different hosts: Access Service Provider using https://host:port/yourapp but in Identity Provider configuration, Assertion Consumer Service endpoint is https://anotherhost:port/sap/saml2/sp/acs/XXX.
  • Different ports: Access Service Provider using https://host:port/yourapp but in Identity Provider configuration, Assertion Consumer Service endpoint is https://host:anotherport/sap/saml2/sp/acs/XXX.
  • Different protocols: Access Service Provider using http://host:port/yourapp but in Identity Provider configuration, Assertion Consumer Service endpoint is https://host:port/sap/saml2/sp/acs/XXX.

If the protocol, host or port is different, it is necessary to export the metadata file once more, using the same protocol, host and port in the URL which is used to access the protected resource, then import the metadata to the Identity Provider once again.

entry.title: Re: Announcing the Winner of "My Most Geeky Hobby” Contest
entry.content: Congratulations @bhavinvyas , @zamichiei1 and @Sandra_Solis 🎉🎉🎉

entry.title: Error During ChangeSet Offline Call
entry.content:
Hi,

from yesterday I have a problem during ChangeSet offline call.

The app return this error: "Error: Attempt to get length of null array"

In the Mobile Client Log Upload i have this:

commit change set error Params: {"serviceUrl":"https://xxxxxxxxxx.hana.ondemand.com/s4-backend-odata/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZSERVICE","offlineEnabled":true} Error: Attempt to get length of null array

The previous week I can call ChangeSet without problem.

I checked every change I made and all seems ok. I do not understand what's the problem. 

I checked all the rules and actions and all seems ok.

I checked also all the arrays in the rules with some alerts and all of them has correct length.

The error seems to come from the Framework.

Someone experienced the same issue ? Any idea ?

ChangeSet action code:

{
    "_Type": "Action.Type.ODataService.ChangeSet",
    "ActionResult": {
        "_Name": "ChangeSetCreateSalesOrder"
    },
    "OnFailure": "/somanagement/Actions/CreateSalesOrderActions/MessageErrorChangeSetCreateSalesOrder.action",
    "OnSuccess": {
        "Name": "/somanagement/Actions/GenericToastMessage.action",
        "Properties": {
            "Message": "$(L,'SalesOrderCreatedSuccess')"
        }
    },
    "Target": {
        "Service": "/somanagement/Services/ServiceV2.service"
    },
    "Actions": [
        "/somanagement/Actions/CreateSalesOrderActions/CreateSalesOrderHead.action",
        "/somanagement/Rules/CreateSalesOrder/CreateSalesOrderItems.js",
        "/somanagement/Rules/CreateSalesOrder/CreateSalesOrderTexts.js"
    ]
}

Thank you,

Angelo.

entry.title: Re: Announcing the Winner of "My Most Geeky Hobby” Contest
entry.content: Congratulations @bhavinvyas @zamichiei1 @Sandra_Solis 👏🏻🏆

entry.title: Re: Is SAP and Sustainability a Myth ?
entry.content: To add the on the db performance, let me break down to another granular level. SAP should push to delete default email generated by workflow approval or process completion after certain days. This will help reducing the data space reduction of CO2 emissions of data centers. I used to be big fan of AI until I learnt how much co2 emitted for one single LLM model execution. There was a popular research done by Harvard business school but later it was removed.

entry.title: Re: Retenção IR Pessoa Física
entry.content:
Boa tarde,

Alguém conseguiu calcular o IRF com redução de base  no momento do pagamento?

Deduzindo o INSS no calculo do IRF momento do pagamento?

entry.title: SAP IAS BP
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have three subaccounts (dev, test, prod) in SAP BTP and want to connect them to SAP IAS/IPS (IAS as proxy and Azure as IdP)

As each customer typically has two IAS tenants (test and prod) and one IPS tenant, I am currently looking for best practices for effectively using IAS tenants.

What is the recommended approach?

  • Should the test IAS tenant be connected to two subaccounts (development and test), and the prod IAS tenant be connected to the production subaccount? Are there any technical restrictions?
  • In which subaccount (dev, test, prod) should IPS be implemented?
  • Or is it preferable to assign each IAS tenant to a single subaccount, resulting in a total of three IAS tenants?

 

I would appreciate any advice

Many Thanks

entry.title: Re: Top Reasons to Attend SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content: @TammyPowlas @KunalBansal @ankurgodre @Kartik_Dua thanks for sharing your playlist with rational. So helpful! #sapchampions #sapmentors

Gracias por su pregunta.

Encontré la siguiente informaciòn: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3115215 y espero que le ayude.

Saludos,

Henrike
